frozen_interpretation.fact: The object-relation post-detector analyzer resolved detector-depth evidence for both bounded request rows without using GT or evaluation labels for action.
frozen_interpretation.agent_inference: The result repairs an observation coverage/depth evidence gap, but it does not establish ObjectNav goal validity because both resolved candidates are evaluation-negative after label join.
frozen_interpretation.blocked_reason: relation_depth_recheck_resolved currently means the candidate can be observed consistently, not that it is the correct goal instance.
frozen_interpretation.required_reframing: Treat object-relation evidence as a nonterminal evidence update and terminal-blocking failure mechanism unless a future non-GT arbitration rule can distinguish resolved-but-negative repeated-object candidates from valid goals.

commit_relation_depth_resolved.reason: The only bounded resolved request rows are evaluation-negative after label join.

commit_box_or_mask_presence.reason: Box and mask presence confirm category-level visibility, not repeated-object goal identity.

commit_high_association_count.reason: Both negative candidates have strong association support, so association count alone is an unsafe terminal signal.

defer_all_after_relation_depth.reason: It preserves safety but cannot test whether relation-aware observation can become useful after additional identity evidence.

use_evaluation_negative_label_as_action_.reason: Evaluation labels may be used only after action-time evidence rows are written.

required_before_terminal_utility: explicit failure case where relation-depth-resolved negative candidates are rejected
comparison against defer-all and simple association/depth/box alternatives
no correctness, success, wrong-goal, valid-candidate, or oracle label use before action rows

user_decision_needed: None for this gate. User judgment is needed only if a later non-GT arbitration rule proposes terminal promotion.
